config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa pagar yang jelas, hak minoritas dan orang yang lemah bisa mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang, bukan dengan menindasnya. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat kuat justru saat individu dilindungi, bukan diabaikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a blanket rule. From an American perspective, that kind of statement makes me uneasy because it can easily become a justification for trampling minority rights, free speech, or due process whenever the majority claims a public good. I do think society sometimes has to limit individual freedom for real harms, like public safety or disease control, but those limits should be narrow, accountable, and temporary. In practice, a healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first and then balancing them carefully against the common good, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia kita tahu betul betapa mudahnya “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan perampasan hak orang kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik, ketertiban, atau bencana, tetapi itu harus dibatasi jelas dan tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk menginjak minoritas atau membungkam suara yang berbeda. Justru masyarakat akan lebih sehat kalau hak individu dilindungi dulu sebagai pagar, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan akuntabel. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik, saya khawatir yang kuat akan makin mudah menentukan nasib yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that wording is too dangerous because it gives majorities and governments a ready-made excuse to override speech, privacy, and due process whenever they claim it is for the common good. I do think there are real cases where society can limit individual freedom, like quarantine rules or emergency public safety measures, but those need strict limits, transparency, and legal checks. In my view, a society is strongest when individual rights are the default and any restriction has to be justified carefully, not when the individual is treated as secondary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia frasa “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa dan merugikan orang kecil. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik, bencana, atau ketertiban yang menuntut pembatasan hak individu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi. Kalau prioritasnya selalu dibalik, saya khawatir yang paling lemah akan paling mudah dikorbankan atas nama mayoritas. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu dulu, lalu mencari kepentingan bersama lewat aturan yang adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American point of view, that sounds too much like a blank check for governments or majorities to override speech, privacy, and due process whenever it is convenient to call something the common good. I do think there are narrow exceptions, like quarantine or emergency safety rules, but those should be tightly limited, transparent, and reviewable. In my view, a free society works best when individual rights are the default and any sacrifice of them has to be justified very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
